sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a two-month history of a red, draining foot ulcer that began after walking barefoot on the beach. He recalls having taken prior medication for a similar condition but did not pick up the prescription. He describes occasional throbbing pain, but not specific pain, and reports increased pressure with touch, but no distinct pain. He states he has not had this type of drainage before. He reports his diabetes is uncontrolled and he does not regularly check his blood sugar. He does not recall his last A1c value.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: A one by two inch circular wound is present on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth metatarsal head. The wound is accompanied by redness, drainage, and edema. There is no evidence of necrosis, odor, or bony exposure. Vital signs are normal. An X-ray of the right foot revealed no evidence of osteomyelitis. The ABI has not been performed.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer. The ulcer is likely secondary to uncontrolled diabetes and potential infection. The patient's risk for further complications is elevated due to uncontrolled diabetes and the presence of the ulcer.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order an ankle brachial index to assess blood flow to the foot. Perform a debridement of the ulcer. Prescribe clindamycin 400 mg twice daily for seven days to treat the infection. Obtain a surgical shoe for the patient to wear while the ulcer heals. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess healing and consider further debridement. Advise the patient to call the office with any concerns or emergencies.
